pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - Joining forces with National Geographic

pageCaption.value: From the deepest oceans to the remotest rainforests and the highest mountains, environmental challenges affect every place on Earth, even the most out-of-the-way areas.

metadescription.value: Rolex and National Geographic have partnered to promote environmental exploration, research, awareness and preservation. Learn more on rolex.org.

text.value:
From the deepest oceans to the remotest rainforests and the highest mountains, environmental challenges affect every place on Earth, even the most out-of-the-way areas. Since 1954, Rolex and National Geographic have joined forces with a clear mission:

Promoting exploration, scientific research, awareness and preservation of these critical environments. Safeguarding the planet and finding solutions to protect it are the main objectives of this collaboration that both entities develop day by day.

pageCaption.value: The clinical microbiologist Hosam Zowawi fights to overcome the resistance of certain bacteria to antibiotics.

metadescription.value: Rolex Young Laureate Hosam Zowawi is studying hospital pathogens to overcome the resistance of certain bacteria to antibiotics. More on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - How technology protects rainforests

pageCaption.value: How can we put a stop to ILLEGAL logging? It would have been tempting to come up with a high-technology solution, but in the Rainforest a simple and practical response is required.

WaPageTitle.value: Analysing the sounds of the Rainforest

metadescription.value: Engineer Topher White, founder of the NGO Rainforest Connection, uses discarded mobile phones to save rainforests. Discover more on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Analysing the sounds of the Rainforest

pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - Stepping back in time by studying the tepuis

pageCaption.value: In the caves hidden under the Tepuis of South America, the geologist Francesco Sauro has discovered clues to the evolution of life on Earth.

WaPageTitle.value: The hidden secrets of the tepuis

metadescription.value: The South American mountains known as tepuis are brimming with information on Earth's prehistorical evolution. Learn more on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: The hidden secrets of the tepuis

pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - Supplying water to farmers in Northern India

pageCaption.value: The Indian engineer Sonam Wangchuk, who won a Rolex Award for Enterprise in 2016, creates artificial cone-shaped glaciers that supply water to the farmers in Ladakh.

metadescription.value: Rolex Laureate Sonam Wangchuk designed artificial glaciers to store water and help valley populations in Northern India. Read more on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - Reversing spinal cord damage with electricity

pageCaption.value: Grégoire Courtine, from France, has conceived a system that enables people with spinal cord injuries to regain their mobility.

metadescription.value: Rolex Laureate and neuroscientist Grégoire Courtine uses wireless technology to help paralysed patients walk again. Learn more on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - Revolutionising methods of malaria diagnosis

pageCaption.value: Brian Gitta, from Uganda, has invented a device that provides a fast diagnosis of malaria and does not require a blood test.

metadescription.value: Using light and magnets, Rolex Laureate Brian Gitta has invented a faster malaria test that does not require drawing blood. Read more on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - New ways to preserve the natural world

pageCaption.value: The Rolex awards for enterprise have recognised various projects dedicated to conservation in 2019.

WaPageTitle.value: Exploring to preserve the natural world

metadescription.value: By making discoveries about the natural world, recipients of the Rolex Awards for Enterprise are helping preserve our planet. Learn more on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Exploring to preserve the natural world

pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - Mission Blue and the preservation of sea life

pageCaption.value: Four decades later, the oceanographer and pioneer in underwater exploration is still at the forefront of the struggle with her Mission Blue initiative.

metadescription.value: Oceanographer and marine biologist Sylvia Earle has created a global alliance to protect marine ecosystems. Learn more about her work on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Sylvia Earle: Pioneer in Underwater Exploration

pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - Turning plastic waste into a valuable product

pageCaption.value: Turning plastic waste into a product of value. This is the mission of Miranda Wang, one of the Laureates of the 2019 Rolex Awards for Enterprise.

metadescription.value: Plastic waste can have positive impact on the planet. Learn how Rolex Laureate Miranda Wang is transforming our waste into raw materials on rolex.org.
